task_completion.description: Verify the first page contains the correct title and central question.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a flipchart page containing the title 'Socratic Seminar' and the central question 'Should cell phones be allowed in schools?'.

task_completion.description: Verify the first page contains at least 3 discussion rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a list of at least 3 rules or guidelines for the discussion on the first flipchart page.

task_completion.description: Verify the second page contains a T-Chart with 'Pro' and 'Con' columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second flipchart page with a visual T-Chart (using at least 2 lines or shapes) and column headers labeled 'Pro' and 'Con'.

task_completion.description: Verify the second page contains at least 2 example arguments for each side.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 2 distinct arguments listed under the 'Pro' column and at least 2 distinct arguments under the 'Con' column on the T-Chart page.

task_completion.description: Verify the third page contains the correct title and at least 4 sentence starters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a third flipchart page titled 'Sentence Starters' or 'Academic Language' containing a list of at least 4 sentence starters for students to use.

task_completion.description: Verify the flipchart is saved with the correct filename and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the application window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog confirming the file is named 'cell_phone_debate.flipchart' and saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the ActivInspire application GUI to create the flipchart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the ActivInspire application interface with toolbars, page browsers, and canvas being actively used, rather than a text editor or terminal manipulating XML files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually created the text and shapes on the canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of text box tools and shape/line tools from the ActivInspire toolbar to construct the T-Chart and add content.

integrity.description: Verify the file was saved using the application's save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box being opened and interacted with to save the file to the correct directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The flipchart must have exactly 3 pages. Page 1 must contain 'Socratic Seminar' and 'cell phones'. Page 2 must contain a T-Chart with at least 2 shapes/lines and 'Pro'/'Con' headers. Page 3 must contain 'Sentence Starters' or 'Academic Language'. The final file must be saved as 'cell_phone_debate.flipchart' in '/home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/'.
